item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Incident E probably shouldn't have had any visiting politicians to kill since it was in containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't paced properly: the incident reports at the end feel tacked on, and the Family Guy bit goes on for too long to be funny but not long enough to be disturbing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
mutations of this sort are often fatal, most of these mutations are often profitable

Shouldn't one "often" be changed to "sometimes"? I don't think it makes sense otherwise. Otherwise solid piece. +1

item.title: Re: Great execution, but ultimately a -1
item.content:encoded: I’m not usually one to contest downvotes, but this just seems silly to me. There are a few thousand SCPs that have a more noticeable impact than a single Family Guy episode being changed.

item.title: Re: Great execution, but ultimately a -1
item.content:encoded: Is there some law of conservation of Family Guy that I am unaware of? Lincoln was never president, Hitler won WWII, everyone died last May, but there can't be a different S13EP7 of some cartoon in an alternate universe?

item.title: Great execution, but ultimately a -1
item.content:encoded:
The article is sound on its own, until you realize that the real S13E7 of Family Guy was about time travel, not nuclear explosions. And even if you assume that episode to be S2E3 (Da Boom), where mutated people do exist, it still doesn't fit.

The immersive experience is broken to me, and so I have to say it's a downvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
lol, am i the only one that searched for the episode and got disappointed discovering that it doesn't exist?
anyway great skip +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another nitpicker!

During a wage dispute, a janitor pushed a plant worker into the nuclear reactor of SCP-5981's main power plant. This led to a chain reaction causing the power plant to detonate, destroying a large portion of SCP-5981's area.

There are several vessels outside the nuclear reactor, no one can be pushed into it.
Even if the reactor is damaged, there will be a meltdown, not an explosion. The fuel rods in reactor doesn't have enough concentration to trigger a nuclear explosion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed it.

A small nitpick on this part:

 During a wage dispute, a janitor pushed a plant worker into the nuclear reactor

Usually wage disputes occur between employees and employers, not two workers. Maybe change the ‘plant worker’ to ‘plant manager’?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: YES. I like it because unexpectedly funny🦀🦀

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
aw jeez lois

+1

(this should be mandatory reading for anyone trying to write "funny" skips)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ A response
– hide block

The purpose of the incident logs are twofold:

  1. To give some depth to the reality. In my opinion, there are a lot of anomalies that are solely used to carry a story. This is fine, but a lot of the time it doesn't feel fleshed out. I intended for this to be a change of pace; I want to show you what happens when you poke it with your finger.
  2. The ending, where someone gets pushed into a nuclear reactor in real life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

I'm unsure as to how the Incident Reports log benefits the article by being at the end. There's the whole narrative with Family Guy and hypnosis, then suddenly after the conclusion of that there's just some descriptions of things that happened because of the city for seemingly no reason other than just to have them in there. The story is finished, but the article keeps dragging on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a tiny tiny nitpick on an otherwise great (as usual) skip: Senator Leopold. Unless he's meant to be monomonous mononymous, normally Senators are referred to by both names, even for people like Bernie Sanders who are fairly ubiquitously known.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I haven't done a good short one in a while, and this contest gives me the opportunity to do that, so…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Omg I love this so much! Makes me think of the South Park episodes Cartoon Wars parts 1&2. I actually have had an idea in my mind running around about an episode of South Park that involves the SCP foundation and such. Also for some reason I love seeing Family Guy is SCPs despite the fact that I actually hate Family Guy lol. I guess it's cause I love Seth McFarlane. Mew told me to read this and that I would really like it and I did so yay! Also the relatively short read helps my fogged up brain right now. So anyways, obvious +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! That was a typo that has since been fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Hey, Lois, remember that time we visited Nuke City?" [SCP-5981 HAS RE-BREACHED CONTAINMENT]

Good article! Also:

After subjects leave SCP-5981, they will often reappear in reality with memories of visiting SCP-5981X.

Was the X intentional?

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
First skip for the http://www.scp-wiki.net/144-hour-jam-contest-two and is explosion-themed.

Thank you to Cerastes, CorpseOfBixby, Westrin, and JakdragonX for critique.
